body:
## Context

Toggling between testnet and mainnet from the wallet header forces a full page reload via `window.location.reload()` in `useSwitchNetwork` (`frontend/src/hooks/useTrading.ts`).

The reload is currently used to re-initialize all in-memory state and local storage-backed portfolio snapshots for the new network. This is jarring UX (loses scroll position, dialog state, in-flight edits, dev console state) and pairs with a TODO at the top of `frontend/src/components/wallet-header.tsx`.

## Problem

- A network switch should not require a full document reload.
- All network-scoped state (queries, portfolio snapshots, local storage entries, websocket subscriptions) needs to be invalidated/reset coherently when the active network changes, without dropping the page.
- Today the reload masks gaps in invalidation — without it, stale testnet data would bleed into mainnet views (and vice versa).
title: Network switch (testnet/mainnet) reloads the page

body:
## Context

We want to know when a rebalance has effectively finished **without** reloading the page and **without** only hoping a fixed delay is long enough (blind wait on a "refreshing" state).

## Directions to explore

- **Polling / fetch**: explicit checks against backend or exchange state until terminal conditions are met.
- **Subscriptions**: a push-style signal (there may be a non-obvious subscription path worth investigating later, including SSR-related patterns).

## Partial failures

If **part** of a multi-step rebalance fails (e.g. during an early "close everything" phase), we need a product decision:

- Example: something sold but a leg did not complete as planned — should we **stop** further rebalancing steps that depend on that outcome, while still allowing unrelated follow-up buys that are not tied to the failed token?
- Document trade-offs and a recommended default.

## Future knob (low priority)

- A **"fail fast"** toggle: abort the rest of the plan as soon as any step fails, vs. best-effort continuation. Likely low priority for now; capture for later design.

## Acceptance (when implemented)

- [ ] Document chosen completion criteria per execution mode (what counts as "done").
- [ ] UX: user-visible terminal state without mandatory full page reload.
- [ ] Document behavior on partial failure (stop / continue / user prompt).

/cc discussion 2026-03-31 (Gleb Dianov, Lev Shatrov)
title: Rebalance: detect completion without reload; handle partial failures (fail-fast later)

body:
## Context
useBeta (frontend/src/pages/Portfolio/hooks/useBeta.ts) computes normalized weights by deriving totalNotional on the client:
- weightsFromPortfolio(portfolio) does Object.values(portfolio).reduce((sum, position) => sum + position.notional, 0)
- then computes weights as position.notional / totalNotional

However, usePortfolioState (frontend/src/pages/Portfolio/hooks/usePortfolioState.ts) already calculates targetTotalNotional (and currentTotalNotional).

## Problem
We currently duplicate work (client-side totalNotional recomputation), even though the same value already exists in state.
This increases CPU work and can also trigger unnecessary query invalidations/refetches when the weights object identity changes.

## Goal
Refactor useBeta to reuse the already computed notional totals from usePortfolioState, making the beta request more efficient.

## Suggested approach
1. Update useBeta API so the caller can provide totalNotional (most likely targetTotalNotional, since index.tsx passes portfolio.targetPortfolio).
2. In weightsFromPortfolio, avoid recomputing totalNotional; instead compute weights as position.notional / providedTotalNotional.
3. Keep filtering out undefined portfolio slots before reading notional/symbol.
4. Consider tightening queryKey to depend on stable primitives (instead of the full weights object reference) to reduce unnecessary refetches.

## Acceptance criteria
- useBeta no longer recomputes totalNotional via a reduce over the entire portfolio.
- Types align with usePortfolioState: Record<string, PortfolioInterface | undefined>.
- Typecheck/lint pass.
- Beta query refetches only when inputs that affect beta actually change.
title: Review useBeta: optimize beta requests by reusing totalNotional

body:
## Context
`frontend/src/pages/Portfolio/components/PositionsPanel/LeverageDialog.tsx` is currently a raw leverage editor:
- It opens via a small `ghost` button and renders an inline modal.
- The slider immediately calls `props.onLeverageChange(props.symbol, leverage)` on every change.
- The only keyboard handling is a `window` `keydown` listener that closes on `Escape`.
- There is no explicit Apply/Cancel workflow, focus management, or accessibility semantics (dialog role/aria, focus trap, initial focus, keyboard support beyond Escape).

## Goal
Design and refactor `LeverageDialog` into a polished, production-ready component with the intended UX and better correctness/safety.

## Requirements (English)
1. **New UI design**
   - Match the app's existing UI patterns (buttons, dialog layout, typography, spacing).
   - Show current leverage, max leverage, and the user-selected leverage.
   - Display a clear affordance for committing or discarding changes.

2. **Apply / Cancel behavior**
   - Slider/input should update a local draft value.
   - `props.onLeverageChange` must be called only when the user clicks **Apply** (or equivalent).
   - **Cancel / Close** should revert the draft and close the dialog.

3. **Validation and clamping**
   - Draft leverage must always stay within `[min, maxLeverage]`.
   - Ensure consistent rounding/step behavior (avoid unexpected values).

4. **Accessibility & keyboard UX**
   - Add proper dialog semantics (`role="dialog"`, `aria-modal`, accessible title/description).
   - Implement focus trapping within the dialog.
   - Set initial focus when the dialog opens (e.g., slider handle or first action button).
   - Ensure keyboard navigation works naturally (Tab/Shift-Tab).
   - Keep the current `Escape`-to-close behavior.

5. **Disabled state correctness**
   - If `props.disabled` is true, the dialog must not open and no interactions should be possible.

6. **Behavioral consistency**
   - Make sure backdrop click and close actions behave consistently.
   - Preserve the existing intent of the `createEffect` `window` listener for Escape (do not refactor it into derived state/event sources incorrectly).

## Notes / Constraints
- The component currently uses an imperative `createEffect` with a `window` `keydown` listener for `Escape` and removes it via `onCleanup`.
- This refactor should improve the UX while keeping correct lifecycle management for that listener.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No `TODO`/placeholder UI remains in LeverageDialog.
- The dialog has Apply/Cancel and does not mutate leverage until Apply.
- Accessibility checks pass (focus trap + dialog semantics).
- Lint/typecheck pass.
title: Refactor LeverageDialog: new design + apply/cancel + accessibility

body:
## Bug description

When opening the per-position leverage control immediately after loading the portfolio page, the UI can throw an error because  has not finished loading yet. This happens if you click the leverage button before  / max leverage values have been fetched.

## Steps to reproduce
1. Start the frontend () and open the portfolio page.
2. As soon as positions are visible (before the leverage limits have fully loaded), click on the leverage button (e.g. , ) in the Positions panel.
3. Observe that an error is thrown in the console / UI related to  being  or invalid.

## Expected behavior
- The leverage UI should handle the initial loading state gracefully:
  - Either disable the leverage button until  is loaded, and/or
  - Render the modal/slider in a safe fallback state (e.g. clamped default) without throwing.

## Additional context / ideas
- Currently,  is fetched separately and may arrive noticeably later than positions.
- It might be possible to batch leverage limits into the same request as positions or reuse existing data from the exchange API to avoid the second round trip.
- Even if we keep the extra request, the Positions panel should guard against   so the user cannot trigger a runtime error just by clicking quickly after load.

Please investigate whether we can (a) harden the leverage UI against missing , and (b) potentially speed this up by fetching positions and leverage caps in a single backend call.
title: Leverage panel crashes when leverage limits are not yet loaded

body:
## Problem
Starting an ingest run currently requires having the HTTP server running and then manually issuing a `curl` request to the ingest endpoint.

This workflow is inconvenient and error-prone:
- It requires remembering the exact endpoint and payload.
- It depends on having the server already running.
- It is hard to script or integrate into automated workflows (e.g. cron, systemd, CI) in a clean way.

## Desired behavior
- Provide a single unified command (e.g. a CLI subcommand or a dedicated binary) to trigger ingest directly, without needing to manually craft `curl` requests.
- The command should:
  - Accept the same parameters as the current ingest HTTP endpoint (or a well-designed subset/superset).
  - Return clear exit codes and logs so it can be used in automation.
  - Either:
    - start ingest end-to-end itself, **or**
    - talk to the running service in a stable, well-defined way (if the architecture requires the server).

## Notes
- Consider how this CLI entrypoint fits into the existing Nix/dev environment (e.g. `cargo run -- ingest ...` or a dedicated binary).
- The goal is to make ingest easy to trigger both manually (local dev) and in automated environments without needing ad-hoc `curl` scripts.
title: Add a single unified command to trigger ingest

body:
## Problem
When an ingest process is started and then crashes or is manually cancelled, the database does not record that the ingest run failed.

As a result, it is often necessary to fully reset/rebuild the database before a new ingest can be started, which is unsafe, inconvenient, and loses useful history.

## Desired behavior
- Each ingest run should be represented in the DB with an explicit status/state (e.g. `running`, `completed`, `failed`, `cancelled`).
- If an ingest crashes or is cancelled, the corresponding record should be updated to `failed` or `cancelled`.
- Subsequent ingests should be able to start without requiring a full DB reset, while still making it obvious in the data that a prior ingest did not complete successfully.

## Notes
- Consider how partial data from a failed ingest is represented and whether it should be safely resumable or must be discarded.
- This issue is about correct state tracking/observability in the DB, not about fixing any specific crash.
title: Mark failed/cancelled ingest runs in DB

body:
Frontend withdrawal flow (request + execute after redeem period). Needs two-phase withdrawal (#128) to land first.

---

**User story**: [Withdraw From Vault](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/024-withdraw-from-vault.md)
title: Vault: withdraw UI

body:
Frontend deposit flow against the deployed devnet program. Can start once #124 deploys and the IDL is published.

---

**User story**: [Deposit Into Vault](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/023-deposit-into-vault.md)
title: Vault: deposit UI against devnet IDL

body:
Rust client wrapping the vault program's instructions. Takes a `Wallet<Address = Pubkey>` for signing. Used by the backend NAV oracle and capital deployment pipeline. Depends on stable IDL + Solana wallet (#99).

---

**User stories**:

- [Deposit Into Vault](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/023-deposit-into-vault.md)
- [Withdraw From Vault](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/024-withdraw-from-vault.md)
title: VaultClient crate (Rust client for vault program)

body:
`request_withdraw` records intent. `execute_withdraw` settles after `redeem_period` elapses -- settles management and performance fees before calculating USDC payout. Burns shares, transfers net USDC to user. Depends on fee math (#127). See VAULT.md.

---

**User story**: [Withdraw From Vault](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/024-withdraw-from-vault.md)
title: Vault: two-phase withdrawal

body:
Management fee: annual percentage of depositor equity, time-accrued, collected via share dilution. Performance fee: percentage of profit above per-depositor HWM with cumulative tracking to prevent double-charging. Both split between manager and platform. Pure math -- can be developed and tested in parallel with other instruction groups. See VAULT.md for formulas.

---

**User story**: [Withdraw From Vault](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/024-withdraw-from-vault.md)
title: Vault: fee math (management + performance)

body:
`transfer_from_treasury`: manager moves USDC out for cross-venue trading via Turnkey wallets. `return_to_treasury`: anyone can return USDC. Treasury balance and `total_assets` are intentionally decoupled -- the vault may hold less USDC than `total_assets` because capital is deployed across venues. See VAULT.md.

---

**User stories**:

- [Deposit Into Vault](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/023-deposit-into-vault.md)
- [Withdraw From Vault](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/024-withdraw-from-vault.md)
title: Vault: treasury management (transfer + return)

body:
Oracle submits total NAV (backend aggregates positions across venues). This is the only way `total_assets` changes outside of deposits/withdrawals. The backend NAV oracle integration depends on this + Solana wallet (#99). See VAULT.md for instruction details.

---

**User stories**:

- [Deposit Into Vault](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/023-deposit-into-vault.md)
- [Withdraw From Vault](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/024-withdraw-from-vault.md)
title: Vault: attest_nav instruction

body:
First instructions that create accounts and accept capital. `initialize_vault` creates the vault PDA, share mint, treasury, and mints virtual shares. `deposit` transfers USDC from user to treasury and mints share tokens. Produces a deployable program with an IDL that unblocks frontend work. See VAULT.md for instruction details.

---

**User story**: [Deposit Into Vault](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/023-deposit-into-vault.md)
title: Vault: initialize_vault + deposit instructions

body:
Set up `programs/vault/` as an Anchor project in the Cargo workspace. Define `VaultState`, `Depositor`, `WithdrawRequest` account structs. Implement share conversion with virtual offset (`VIRTUAL_SHARES = 10^6`, `VIRTUAL_ASSETS = 1`) -- pure math, fully unit-testable. See VAULT.md for full type definitions.

---

**User stories**:

- [Deposit Into Vault](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/023-deposit-into-vault.md)
- [Withdraw From Vault](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/024-withdraw-from-vault.md)
title: Vault: Anchor scaffold + account types + share math

body:
## Target Outcome

Cross-chain bridge signing via Turnkey for deBridge transfers between Solana and Hyperliquid.

## Desired Behaviors

- Signs bridge transactions on the source chain (Solana or EVM)
- Bridge operations use the same wallet instances as the source chain — no separate "bridge wallet"

## Invariants

- Uses existing `TurnkeyEvmWallet` or `TurnkeySolanaWallet` depending on source chain
- Policy enforcement via Turnkey ensures only whitelisted bridge contracts

## Dependencies

- Requires both Turnkey EVM (#98) and Turnkey Solana (#99) wallets
title: Turnkey deBridge wallet

body:
## Target Outcome

`TurnkeySolanaWallet` implements the `Wallet` trait for Solana (vault operations, Jupiter swaps).

## Desired Behaviors

- Signs serialized Solana transaction bytes via Turnkey's `sign_raw_payload` API
- Returns `solana_signature::Signature`
- Account address is `solana_pubkey::Pubkey`

## Invariants

- Behind a `solana` feature flag
- Payload is `Vec<u8>` (serialized transaction bytes)
- Uses the same `TurnkeyWalletError` as the EVM implementation

## Dependencies

- Requires Turnkey EVM wallet (#98) to establish the Turnkey integration pattern

---

**User story**: [Authenticate Portfolio Ownership By Solana Pubkey](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/015-authenticate-portfolio-ownership-by-solana-pubkey.md)
title: Turnkey Solana wallet

body:
## Target Outcome

`TurnkeyEvmWallet` implements the `Wallet` trait for EVM chains (Hyperliquid signing).

## Desired Behaviors

Signs EVM payloads (pre-hashed `B256`) via Turnkey's `sign_raw_payload` API

* Returns `alloy_primitives::PrimitiveSignature` with correct `r`, `s`, `v` fields
* Stores the Turnkey organization ID as a newtype (`OrganizationId`)
* Account address is stored at construction time (one wallet = one address)

## Invariants

* Behind `turnkey` feature flag — enabling it pulls in `turnkey_client`, `turnkey_api_key_stamper`, `alloy-primitives`
* Payload encoding uses `HexadecimalBytes`, hash function uses `NoHash` (caller pre-hashes)
* API errors are mapped to a typed `TurnkeyWalletError`, never string errors

## Dependencies

* Requires wallet trait ([DCARTEL-37](https://linear.app/data-cartel/issue/DCARTEL-37/wallet-trait-crate-architecture)) to be merged first
title: Turnkey EVM wallet

body:
## Target Outcome

A `wallet` crate under `crates/` that defines the canonical pattern for all future domain crates in the system.

## Desired Behaviors

* **Trait-based domain boundary**: The crate exposes a `Wallet` trait modeling signing + address retrieval. Consumers depend on the trait, never concrete implementations.
* **Feature-gated implementations**: Each implementation (turnkey, mock) lives behind a feature flag. Dependants enable only the flags they need.
* **Associated types for chain specificity**: Address, Payload, Signature, Error are associated types — consumers constrain via `W: Wallet<Address = alloy_primitives::Address>` rather than runtime dispatch.
* **One wallet = one chain = one address**: A Turnkey org with EVM + Solana accounts produces two wallet instances.
* **Zero cross-domain dependencies**: The wallet crate depends on no other domain crates.

## Invariants

* /Enabling no features compiles only the trait + common types
* Enabling `mock` provides a deterministic test implementation
* The trait has no chain-specific dependencies — chain types come from implementations
* The crate follows the workspace lint configuration (zero warnings under clippy pedantic + nursery)

## Scope

* Workspace conversion (root Cargo.toml gains `[workspace]`)
* `crates/wallet/` crate with `Wallet` trait
* Mock implementation behind `mock` feature flag
* Documentation of the crate architecture pattern in [CLAUDE.md](<http://CLAUDE.md>)
* [SPEC.md](<http://SPEC.md>) crate mapping update: `signing | Signer | privy, mock` → `wallet | Wallet | turnkey, mock`

---

**User story**: [Authenticate Portfolio Ownership By Solana Pubkey](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/015-authenticate-portfolio-ownership-by-solana-pubkey.md) (preparatory infra)
title: wallet trait + crate architecture

body:
Right now the frontend’s notion of the portfolio state is not reliably in sync with what actually happens on Hyperliquid after a rebalance.

What currently happens:

- We construct a rebalance payload that includes:
  - modified positions (e.g. BTC from current notional to target notional),
  - `deleted` positions (full closes, e.g. APE with percentage → 0 and `status: "deleted"`).
- The Hyperliquid client:
  - processes `deleted` positions first via `closePosition` (reduce-only market close),
  - then rebalances the rest (`processPosition` / `placeOrder`), logging a purple `[Rebalance] Deletions:` table and a purple `[Rebalance] Order preview:` table.
- On `onSuccess` of the mutation we currently:
  - assume the orders “logically” went through,
  - trigger a refetch of positions and account summary (with a small polling window), and
  - rebuild the portfolio state and staged changes from those refetched positions.

The problem:

- In practice, it’s very easy for the UI to show a “completed” rebalance while the actual Hyperliquid positions remain unchanged or only partially updated:
  - deleted positions can still be present after a “successful” rebalance (e.g. APE remains open even though it was marked `deleted` and logged in `[Rebalance] Deletions`),
  - modified positions can keep their old notional (e.g. BTC stays near the old size even though the order preview clearly shows a large `deltaNotional`).
- Our current logic treats the HTTP/ccxt call success + a timed refetch as if it were proof that “the portfolio is now in the target state”, which is not always true:
  - orders can be rejected, partially filled, or still in-flight on the exchange side,
  - ccxt fetches are snapshot-based and may race with order processing,
  - we don’t have a strong source of truth that “all relevant orders have finished and the on-chain / exchange state is settled”.

Why this is dangerous:

- The UI can present a “green” state (no staged changes, rebalance button idle) while the actual positions on Hyperliquid are still in the *previous* configuration or some inconsistent intermediate state.
- For a hedging / risk-management tool this is particularly bad: the user may think they are hedged or fully closed when they are not.
- Staged changes semantics become unreliable, because we clear or re-base them based on an assumption of sync rather than confirmed execution.

What we need instead:

- A robust way to know when orders are truly done and the positions snapshot is trustworthy. Concretely:
  - Use Hyperliquid’s websocket (or another streaming mechanism, if available) to subscribe to:
    - order lifecycle (submitted / open / partially filled / filled / canceled / rejected),
    - position updates for the relevant symbols.
  - Track **per-rebalance** the set of symbols we touched (from the payload) and wait until:
    - all related orders for those symbols reach a terminal state (filled / canceled / rejected), and
    - the positions stream shows a new stable state for those symbols (no further updates for some short window).
- Only after this “convergence” should we:
  - rebuild `initialPortfolio` / `selectedTokens` from the latest positions,
  - clear staged changes for the successfully converged symbols,
  - flip the UI out of the “Rebalancing” / “pending” state.

Acceptance criteria:

- When I rebalance:
  - deleting a position (e.g. APE) and resizing another (e.g. BTC) results in the on-screen portfolio matching **exactly** what Hyperliquid reports, without ghost positions or stale notionals.
- The UI never shows “no staged changes” / “rebalance complete” while:
  - a position that was supposed to be deleted is still open on Hyperliquid, or
  - a position that was supposed to be resized is still at (approximately) its old notional.
- The implementation should be based on a streaming / event-driven confirmation (websocket or equivalent), not just timed HTTP refetch plus optimistic assumptions.
title: Portfolio state is not reliably synced with actual Hyperliquid positions

body:
When attempting to fully close the portfolio (set all target weights to 0%), the frontend validation blocks the rebalance with the error:

> Sum of weights is below 100% by 100.0%. Add allocations.

This makes it impossible to use the current portfolio UI to fully exit all positions into cash.

## Expected behavior

- User can set all portfolio weights to 0% and submit a rebalance.
- For all existing positions, the system emits full-close orders (reduce positions to zero).
- The total-weights validation does not block this specific \"close all\" scenario.

## Actual behavior

- When all weights are set to 0%, the validation message appears:
  \`Sum of weights is below 100% by 100.0%. Add allocations.\`
- The Rebalance button remains disabled, so a full portfolio close is not possible.

## Notes

- The \"sum of weights must be ~100%\" rule is useful for normal rebalance flows, but it should allow an exception when:
  - all target weights are 0%, and
  - there is at least one existing position in the initial portfolio (we are closing, not reallocating).
- The staged changes logic already builds proper delete/close operations for existing positions; the blocker is only the high-level validation in \`usePortfolioState\` (blockingReasons/disableSubmit)."
title: Cannot fully close portfolio due to 100% weights validation

body:
Currently, deploying non-master branches requires editing the CI deploy gate each time (adding branch names to the if condition). This is error-prone and wastes time.

**Needed:**
- A staging environment (separate DigitalOcean droplet or same host with different port/config)
- CI deploys to staging on pushes to designated branches (e.g. any PR branch, or a staging branch)
- CI deploys to production only on master
- Staging should use the same NixOS config but with a different hostname/IP

This unblocks testing deployment changes, backend features, and infrastructure without touching production.
title: Add staging environment for pre-production deployments

body:
POST /beta returns a beta value without indicating whether the underlying data is stale. If ingestion hasn't run in days, the beta is computed from old prices.

**Needed:**
- Check the most recent candle timestamp before computing beta
- Return staleness info alongside the beta value (e.g. `{ beta: 0.59, data_age_hours: 2 }`)
- Frontend should warn if data is older than a threshold (e.g. 24h)
title: Data freshness validation on beta endpoint

body:
Ingestion is currently one-shot — triggered manually via POST /ingest. For beta and future analytics to be useful, data must stay current.

**Needed:**
- Periodic ingestion trigger (daily at minimum, hourly preferred)
- Could be systemd timer, cron job, or internal scheduler (apalis is already a dependency)
- Must handle failures gracefully (retry, alert on consecutive failures)
- Ingestion status should be queryable (GET /ingestion/status already exists)

Without this, beta values will go stale immediately after deployment.
title: Scheduled data ingestion (cron)

body:
The rebalancer currently only handles perps. Unified portfolio management needs spot:

- Hyperliquid spot venue integration
- Combined notional and weight calculations across perps + spot
- Single rebalance action across both instrument types

See ROADMAP.md Phase 4, SPEC.md Domain Architecture > Spot Trading.

---

**User story**: [Trade Hyperliquid Spot Positions](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/030-trade-hyperliquid-spot-positions.md)
title: Hyperliquid spot trading integration

body:
Before executing trades, users should see the impact of staged changes:

- Add/remove positions, adjust weights and leverage in a staging area
- Instantly compare staged vs current portfolio: factor exposures, risk metrics, historical performance
- Show the specific trades needed to rebalance from current to staged target
- Only execute when the user commits the staged changes

See ROADMAP.md Phase 3, SPEC.md Core Workflow > Stage, Simulate.

---

**User story**: [Simulate Staged Portfolio Metrics](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/029-simulate-staged-portfolio-metrics.md)
title: Staged trade simulation: preview portfolio changes before execution

body:
Users need to find assets by factor characteristics when constructing portfolios:

- Rank/filter assets by beta to BTC (or other benchmarks)
- Momentum ranking (returns over configurable periods)
- Carry ranking (funding rates — positive = longs pay shorts)
- Volatility ranking
- Sharpe ratio (risk-adjusted returns)

See ROADMAP.md Phase 3, SPEC.md Core Workflow > Screen.

---

**User story**: [Screen Perps By Factor](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/028-screen-perps-by-factor.md)
title: Asset screener: rank by beta, momentum, carry, volatility

body:
Portfolio-level risk metrics beyond beta:

- Monte Carlo simulation of portfolio returns
- VaR/CVaR at configurable confidence levels
- Historical drawdown analysis
- Correlation matrix across held positions
- Effective number of bets (true diversification accounting for correlations)

See ROADMAP.md Phase 2, SPEC.md Analytics Capabilities > Risk Engine.

---

**User story**: [Show Risk Analytics For Active Portfolio](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/027-show-risk-analytics-for-active-portfolio.md)
title: Portfolio risk analytics: VaR, CVaR, correlation matrix

body:
## User Flow

1. User sets their target portfolio composition (e.g., 50% BTC, 30% ETH, 20% SOL)
2. Time passes, prices move, positions drift from target
3. User reopens the tool
4. User sees how much their current allocation has diverged from their target
5. User clicks Rebalance to restore their target composition

## Problem

The UI currently doesn't clearly distinguish between:
- **Target portfolio**: the user's intended allocation
- **Current portfolio**: what's actually on the exchange right now

This makes it hard to:
- See at a glance how much drift has occurred
- Understand what changes will happen when hitting Rebalance
- Know which positions have deltaInsufficient (too small to adjust)

## Proposed Solution

Show both target and current states clearly, along with the delta between them.

---

**User story**: [Compare Target vs Current Portfolio](https://github.com/data-cartel/moneymentum/blob/master/user-stories/026-compare-target-vs-current-portfolio.md)
title: Clearer distinction between target vs current portfolio composition
